page: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. This specific configuration features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

A defining characteristic of this instance is its memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This high memory density is designed for memory-intensive workloads. The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options, for which the instance is fully capable. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the E32-8s_v6 in the average to strong tiers. It demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2146.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 31.3 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8313.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 7989.26. Memory bandwidth is measured at 613.46 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 65.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For database operations, the instance handles 11.29 million Redis SET operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the E32-8s_v6 is its low core count relative to its massive memory footprint, which makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory capacities without high parallel CPU demands. However, it exhibits weaker performance in heavy computational tasks, such as medium-to-large LLM text generation, where it achieves only 6.37 tokens/sec on a 7B model. This profile is highly suited for memory-cached databases, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing tasks that do not require local ephemeral storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. A key highlight is its massive memory capacity of 256.0 GB, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, though it lacks local storage and onboard GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2146.0 and 31.3 GB/sec gzip compression, alongside solid database capabilities, processing 11.29 million Redis SET operations per second. This memory-optimized configuration is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications with moderate CPU requirements, making it an excellent fit for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, ideal for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an exceptional RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz
Massive 256.0 GB of RAM providing 32.0 GB per vCPU
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Strong single-core performance with 2146.0 Geekbench score
Best suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
